ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301103 covers green tea beverage as a category; no specific entry for ITO EN Oi Ocha bottled product. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000 covers tea as a broad category. No specific code for bottled unsweetened Japanese green tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 refers to green tea as a substance; used as closest parent for this bottled ready-to-drink product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 500 ml bottle of Oi Ocha, as commonly sold at convenience stores and food courts in Singapore and Japan.

nutrient_source.title: ITO EN Oi Ocha Green Tea — Nutrition Facts Panel; corroborated by USDA FoodData Central: Tea, green, brewed, regular (FDC ID 171917)
nutrient_source.organization: ITO EN Ltd. / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ITO EN Oi Ocha is an unsweetened bottled green tea. Nutrient values are based on USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171917 (brewed green tea, regular) which closely matches the product composition. The product label confirms 0 kcal, 0g protein, 0g fat, 0g carbohydrate per 100 ml. Sodium is approximately 2 mg per 100 ml per product label. Values are consistent with USDA brewed green tea data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of beverages — plain unsweetened green tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI assigned by convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: Unsweetened green tea contains 0g carbohydrate per 100 ml and therefore has no measurable glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a numeric GI to zero-carbohydrate beverages. Per Project Veritas v2 convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero), gi_category is low, and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 2 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Oi Ocha unsweetened green tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its negligible sodium content; regular green tea consumption has been associated with modest reductions in blood pressure in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content; no glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Oi Ocha is an unsweetened, zero-carbohydrate beverage that does not raise blood glucose and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as a calorie-free hydration option; green tea polyphenols (catechins) may also have modest insulin-sensitising effects per peer-reviewed literature.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of green tea catechins (EGCG)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Oi Ocha contains no fat or cholesterol and provides green tea catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which have been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and improved endothelial function in cardiovascular research; it is a heart-friendly beverage choice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oi Ocha (お~いお茶) is a flagship ready-to-drink unsweetened Japanese green tea produced by ITO EN, one of Japan's largest tea manufacturers. The name translates loosely to 'Hey, tea!' in Japanese. It is brewed from high-quality Japanese green tea leaves, primarily sencha, and is bottled without any added sugar, sweeteners, or artificial flavourings. The beverage has a clean, slightly astringent, and mildly vegetal flavour profile characteristic of sencha green tea, with a light golden-green colour. It is widely available across Japan and in Asian markets and food courts globally, including Singapore, where it is sold at Japanese restaurant chains such as Sushi Express. Per 100 ml, Oi Ocha contains approximately 0 kcal, 0g protein, 0g fat, and 0g carbohydrate, making it a zero-calorie hydration option. It contains trace sodium (approximately 2 mg per 100 ml) and naturally occurring potassium. The product contains vitamin C (ascorbic acid) added as a preservative to maintain freshness. Clinically, Oi Ocha is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its zero sugar, zero fat, and very low sodium profile. Green tea catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), present in the brewed tea, have been studied for antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potential metabolic benefits.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Oi Ocha?
Is Oi Ocha suitable for diabetics?
What is Oi Ocha made of?
Is Oi Ocha the same as green tea?
Can I drink Oi Ocha if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of green tea?

confidence_reason: Oi Ocha is a well-known commercial product by ITO EN with a publicly available nutrition facts panel confirming zero calories, zero macronutrients, and trace sodium. Values are corroborated by USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171917 for brewed green tea. Product identity, ingredients, and nutritional profile are well-established.
notes: Oi Ocha is a zero-calorie unsweetened bottled green tea. energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is a non-caloric beverage containing no macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as brewed green tea contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed green tea contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is an unsweetened beverage with no added sugars or carbohydrates. Confirmed zero per ITO EN product label and USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171917. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source database for this product. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source database. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as the exact added amount is not disclosed in the USDA reference entry (product label notes ascorbic acid as a preservative but does not quantify per 100 ml). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Potassium value of 8 mg per 100 ml is sourced from USDA FDC ID 171917 for brewed green tea. The product is served at Sushi Express - Funan on GrabFood Singapore at SGD 2.50.
